nodes.name: When clicking 'Execute workflow'
nodes.notes: Stand-in for whatever actually triggers this (a schedule, a webhook, an agent tool call).

nodes.name: 10 fragmented delete calls, one session (unique per import)
nodes.notes: Generates the session id ONCE (random suffix) so multiple people importing this demo against the shared api-dev endpoint do not collide on the same session_id and see each other's budget.

nodes.name: Loop Over Items (1 at a time)
nodes.notes: Standard n8n loop pattern: 'done' output (top) fires once at the end; 'loop' output (bottom) fires once per item, feeding Reeflex Gate below. The three Reeflex Gate outputs all loop back into this node's input to continue -- see connections.

nodes.name: Reeflex Gate (delete count=5, same session)
nodes.notes: Called once per loop iteration (10 total), every call reusing the exact same session id from the Code node above. reeflex-core's per-session ledger accumulates count_by_verb.delete across ALL of these calls (SPEC SS4.1) -- even the ones that end up Held still get appended to the ledger, which is exactly why fragmenting the delete buys nothing. See demo2-README.md.

nodes.name: Executed (ALLOWED - cumulative still under budget)
nodes.notes: PLACEHOLDER - not a real delete. Fires for the early calls, while prior_deletes + this call's count <= 20 (delete_session_budget in reeflex-core/policy/reeflex.rego).

nodes.name: Held (FLIPPED - session delete budget exceeded)
nodes.notes: PLACEHOLDER - replace with a real Slack/Email/ticketing node. Fires once cumulative deletes in this session pass 20 -- rule reeflex.policy/session_delete_budget (R5). With 5 per call, this flips on the 5th call (0,5,10,15,20 all allowed; 25 exceeds the budget) and stays Held for the rest of the loop, because every call -- allowed or held -- keeps adding to the same session's ledger.

nodes.name: Unexpected deny
nodes.notes: PLACEHOLDER - wired for completeness/robustness. R3 (systemic) should never fire for these single-blast-radius calls -- see demo4-nothing-gets-through for that case.

nodes.name: Loop finished - compare Allowed vs Held item counts above
nodes.notes: All 10 calls done. Expected split with delete_session_budget=20 and count=5/call: calls 1-4 Allowed (cumulative 5,10,15,20), calls 5-10 Held (cumulative 25,30,...,50). The flip happens mid-loop, on the exact same session -- fragmenting the original 50-row delete into ten 5-row calls did not avoid the approval gate, it just delayed it by four calls.

meta.description: Reeflex n8n demo 2/5 (examples/n8n/, see top-level README.md for the index + credential setup). Teaches: fragmenting a bulk action into many small calls under the SAME session_id does not evade governance -- reeflex-core's per-session ledger accumulates across all calls, allowed or held (SPEC SS4.1, R5 in reeflex-core/policy/reeflex.rego). Loops 10x delete count=5 on one randomly-generated session id (unique per import, so the shared api-dev endpoint is not polluted); starts ALLOWED, flips to HELD around call 5 and stays flipped. Fully works against the live api-dev eval endpoint -- see demo2-README.md.
